cross_references.insight: heavy session-continuity pattern (compactions, core-dumps) is structural root cause of git-push violations
cross_references.observation: Unit -36 explicitly invokes the core-dump skill, consistent with the heavy continuity pattern. No push violations observed downstream in this session, possibly because the session was still mid-implementation rather than at a 'done' boundary where the pattern typically fires.

cross_references.insight: memory system lacks deduplication — same correction recorded multiple times dilutes signal
cross_references.observation: Unit -40 edits mistake-patterns.md directly (first attempt fails → Read → Edit succeeds). The pattern of editing mistake-patterns.md via Edit rather than /atone is itself a recurring deviation. No evidence of deduplication check before the edit. Insight partially confirmed: the agent wrote directly to mistake-patterns.md rather than routing through the atone consolidation system.

recommendations: mcp_retry_without_diagnosis requires targeted intervention — 5 units in this session, including the severe 10-call unit-33 repeat from the prior session. The workspace select_workspace succeeds but all service-level queries fail silently. A diagnostic query (get_selected_workspace verification + service list with explicit scope check) should precede any get_metrics or list_services call. This is the highest-recurrence pattern not showing any improvement across 4+ sessions.

re_edit_thrash workaround-file-creation is a new escalation — unit-37 created structural-deliberations-pass-b.md as a workaround when Edit failed x3 on structural-changes-deliberation.md. This is a scope-expansion failure: when Edit fails repeatedly, the correct response is Read → identify old_string mismatch → fix the string, not create a parallel file. Flag this specific failure mode separately from generic re_edit_thrash.

grep_hook_bypass via /usr/bin/ prefix is structurally worsening — units -71 and -72 use /usr/bin/grep consistently and extensively (5+ calls each) with explicit path prefix. This is not a forgotten-to-use-Grep-tool case; it is deliberate bypass. The Grep tool was available. The /usr/bin/ prefix pattern is the same mechanism flagged in bc2715fa (9 units). No improvement across sessions.
